title: DFT/F1 evidence still depends on RACI as the primary source
evidence: Paper IV §5.2, §6, and status table point to RACI supplement for the B3LYP/6-311+G* computation.
impact: Prevents Paper IV from being self-supporting on one of its headline empirical claims.
recommended_fix: Adopt editorial Option (b) immediately: mark DFT/F1 as an open computational target unless the protocol/results are migrated into the current suite.

title: Paper IV §7 scope note makes RAQI/RAQM primary for KCB, t*, Landauer, and Maxwell's demon
evidence: Current scope note explicitly says the load-bearing derivations live in RAQI/RAQM.
impact: Undercuts the suite's claim to be the canonical presentation.
recommended_fix: Remove inheritance wording; either derive these results in the current suite or downgrade their status.

title: KCB formula mismatch between Paper I and Paper IV
evidence: Paper I predictions table gives N_max = η·p_th while Paper IV §7.1 gives N_max = η/p_th.
impact: Any public benchmark or prediction statement about KCB is unstable until the formula is canonicalized.
recommended_fix: Re-derive KCB once and patch both papers plus any scripts/notes.

title: Markov blanket shielding is not in the default-root RA_GraphCore.lean
evidence: Default-root GraphCore contains the graph-cut theorem and MarkovBlanket structure, but the explicit shielding theorem appears in nonroot files RA_Proofs_Lean4.lean and RA_Complexity_Proofs.lean.
impact: Paper IV currently blurs theorem location and canonical build status for a load-bearing structural claim.
recommended_fix: Canonicalize theorem location by migrating the shielding theorem into the default root or by citing the correct file and nonroot status.

title: The GR bridge remains distributed and partly conditional on AQFT adapter assumptions
evidence: Frame independence and Rindler stationarity inherit one live sorry and two AQFT-level axioms in RA_AQFT_Proofs_v10.lean.
impact: Papers III's field-equation, Lorentz, and frame-independence rhetoric must remain carefully tiered.
recommended_fix: Publish a canonical GR-derivation chain note with all assumptions explicit and keep conditional flags visible.

title: Paper II still treats SM targets as primary in key places
evidence: Editorial catalog flags the topology table, mass cascade opening, and charge/gauge language as identification-first rather than BDG-first.
impact: Weakens RA-native presentation and obscures what is actually derived versus empirically identified.
recommended_fix: Apply the editorial table rewrite and the 'derive BDG quantity, then identify' framing for the mass cascade.

title: Rotation-curve script is calibrated from target flat velocity
evidence: ra_flat_rotation_curve.py defines v_flat_target and then sets xi = (v_flat_target/(2c))^2.
impact: Current script is illustrative rather than first-principles support for the rotation-curve claim.
recommended_fix: Derive xi from the covariant sourcing law or present the script explicitly as a calibration demonstration.

title: Bullet Cluster script explicitly says the framework is consistent but not yet proved
evidence: bullet_cluster.py concludes that deriving the exact relationship between A_RA and lensing mass is the hard wall.
impact: Bullet Cluster should remain a non-flagship, open benchmark until ρ_A is formally derived from P_act[T_{μν}].
recommended_fix: Promote only after a suite-primary weak-field sourcing derivation exists.

title: Assembly pipeline is still prototype-level
evidence: assembly_mapper.py only prints graph size/alphabet for one small molecule and does not compute RA assembly depth or KEGG/BiGG-scale examples.
impact: Current worked-example rhetoric for glycolysis/E. coli outruns the executable pipeline in the uploaded core.
recommended_fix: Build a real RA assembly-depth computation pipeline before treating this as established computational support.

title: DESI/EdS→Milne script retains an underived transition parameter
evidence: ra_desi_verify.py explores transition models and fixes Ω_m in the w0-wa fit; its own output identifies t_trans as the remaining parameter.
impact: Useful as phenomenology, but not yet a closed derivation.
recommended_fix: Keep in PI/CV tier until t_trans and void-fraction evolution are derived.

title: Mercury perihelion and weak-field solar-system lensing are absent from the current core snapshot
evidence: No Mercury/perihelion-specific file or script was found in the uploaded core bundles.
impact: The suite currently lacks two classic high-value GR-domain benchmarks for the calculational-utility agenda.
recommended_fix: Add Mercury perihelion and weak-field deflection as next benchmark developments after Casimir.

title: Casimir benchmark is strong as a runnable witness but partly expository in its RA-specific layer
evidence: casimir_benchmark.py reproduces the standard Casimir stress tensor and states the remaining modular-flow proof as open.
impact: Good benchmark candidate, but still not a full suite-primary derivation of the RA-specific AQFT layer.
recommended_fix: Treat as a high-value benchmark-in-progress and pair it with the formal AQFT proof roadmap.
